comment.body:
yes JFinn,
and I'm very glad.
I know you come from RT :))
he was like always spreading up or dpwn biases. 
It is ok when somebody has a 80%+ accuracy, but thats extremely rare, not too mention that accuracy rate also fluctuates, and you never know when it is the rest 20%
So it's like... my trades were 77% accurate during october, but only 33% accurate over november... but the question is "how many trades? 1000 trades in november and only 80 trades in october?" very-very context dependent.
not to mention, with an accuracy rate of 77% you can still have a red month, if your losses are bigger than your wins... and that 33% rate also can be green for the same reason.
So accuracy  rate is a furu bullshit.

This is a very toxic mentality that must be throw out of the window.
This is one of the reasons why i don't give intraday signals anymore. 
(the others is the difference in style)
This discord-copytrading mentality is just a "sell shovel during gold rush" business thing.

The lesson one must learn is risk management.
How to set stop-loss, 
how to determine your trade size,
and when to leave or hold a certain trade.
(and which instrument to use)
I have posts on that, but will give more soon, after holiday... very important. 

Market can do anything.
You have to be ready for anything. Different scenarios have different probabilities, but only one will happen at the end. 
The leves give you informations.
Thats what an analyst do: finding the levels that matters and break it down what they mean.
From that, you can determine what dynamics and patterns you are looking for for certain moves to happen. Some use RSI and MACD, some use ordeflow metrics, others use realtime quant methods etc. 
It doesn't really matter at the end of the day. The only thing that matters is to understand the tool you are using. To be familiar with it.
And to make it like a machine: don't hesitate to quit when you know you have to quit, and don't panic sell when you know you should hold.
This is why one has to make a plan before the session. 
It requires preparation.

This is why, I always ask the members to make and sahre their plans here before the opening, but a lot of them does not do it. Either bcs they don't plan anything, or bcs they are occuped... 
But thats a very good learning experience.
